description: Discover the rich tapestry of black culture through our vibrant email template. Immerse yourself in the artistic realms of black history, music, dance, and song. This template showcases the contributions of black artists in shaping arts and culture. It's a celebration of the diversity and depth of black culture, perfect for newsletters or commemorating events like Juneteenth. The design incorporates unique elements of art and design, making it a visually stunning tribute to black history. This is more than just an email template; it is a platform to honor and embrace the profound impact of black culture on our shared history.
short_description: Explore black culture with our vibrant email template, highlighting the significant contributions of black artists in arts, music, dance, and song. Perfect for newsletters or Juneteenth events, this design is a visually stunning tribute to black history. Embrace the richness of black culture today.

designer.description:
Graphic Design is at the heart of my story, but I’ve been known to write myself a new part now and again… happily and frequently embracing such roles as: Project Manager, Strategic Thinker, Visual Communicator, Copy Writer, Brand Stylist and Content Curator... magician.

Design is where my passion and my purpose intersect, and providing clients with “the goods” to help their businesses soar is a pretty amazing thing to hop outta’ bed and do each day!

html_data:
ART | SONG| DANCE
 

06.19.1865

 

JUNE

TEENTH

 

Honour and embrace Black culture through art, song and dance.

 
Learn more
 

Most people are familiar with the celebration and history behind Independence Day. While the United States became free in 1776, Black people weren’t freed from slavery at that time.

 

In fact, Black people were not declared free for another 87 years. And even still, it took an additional two years beyond Abraham Lincoln’s signing of the Emancipation Proclamation for the last enslaved people to be freed in Galveston, TX.

 

June 19, 1865 – Juneteenth – is the oldest nationally celebrated commemoration of the ending of slavery in the United States.

Song

Embrace Storytelling
